"We need to make spring so that it is really noticeable that Ukraine is moving towards victory," said Selenskyj in his evening video message. In this context, he once again argued for rapid supply of weapons from abroad.Selenskyj referred to the massive losses that Russia is currently experiencing on the front in eastern Ukraine. This phase must now be used, he said: "Our task is to ensure that there (in Russia) is less and less an effort to keep Ukrainian territories occupied."For the first time, the Bundeswehr allows insight into its training program: 70 Ukrainian soldiers are trained on the Patriot air defense system. According to Federal Defence Minister Boris Pistorius, no new commitments from other countries for Leopard 2A6 tanks have been made at a meeting of the so-called tank coalition. "There we will not reach the battalion strength," the SPD politician admitted. According to Pistorius' information, almost 30 Leopard 2A4 have now come together for a battalion. On the other hand, however, only the 14 Leopard 2A6 and 3 of these tanks from Portugal, promised by Germany, are standing. Sweden still checks whether they could provide tanks and to what extent, he said. A battalion is usually equipped with 31 tanks in Ukraine. On Tuesday, Pistorius had already said the situation .The importance of the remaining commitments of other countries relativeed Pistorius. He said that there were more than 120 Leopard 1A5 who wanted to send companies to Ukraine by the beginning of next year. The Ukrainian soldiers in the heavily fought town of Bachmut hold their positions according to the Ukrainian President Wolodymyr Selenskyj, despite the "most difficult" location on site. Selenskyj said at a press conference with Swedish Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson in Kiev. "And our fortress lives." The situation in Bachmut in the east of the country is at the moment »the most difficult« nationwide, cleared Selenskyj and called as another critical place Wuhledar south of Bachmut.The armament company Rheinmetall intends to deliver the ammunition for the Gepard anti-aircraft armor from summer onwards. "The first cartridges are delivered in the summer of 2023", reports Rheinmetall. A total of 300,000 cartridges are to be produced in two versions. The ammunition is determined according to the company's information to support the Ukrainian armed forces deploying the armies. The order value was in the low three-digit million euro area.Federal Defense Minister () said on Tuesday that the contracts were signed. For Ukraine, it is currently about not losing the airspace to the Russian Air Force and not having to accept more bombardments and attacks on critical infrastructure, the Minister had explained. According to information from the Ministry, since the summer, Germany has delivered 32 Gepard aircraft defense tanks to Ukraine, and five others will follow until the end of February. According to the air force, the training of Ukrainian soldiers at the Patriot air defense system in Germany is rapidly progressing. The Ukrainians were highly motivated and often already experienced in use, so that it is “faster than expected”, said the commander of the German training association. The course for about 70 men runs for about two weeks at a Bundeswehr site, which is not to be called for for safety reasons.Patriot (»Phased Array Tracking Radar for Intercept on Target«) is one of the most modern anti-aircraft systems in the world. This can combat enemy aircraft, ballistic missiles and march missiles. At a distance of about 100 kilometres and up to 30 kilometres, the rifle missiles can reach targets in an imaginary bell – depending on the used steering missile. Germany, together with the ones, had agreed to leave Ukraine with a system of modern anti-aircraft defence against Russian attacks, which are increasingly aimed at the country's civilian infrastructure. The military administration in has informed that six Russian balloons had been sighted over the Ukrainian capital. The air defense has shot most of it, the authority informed on its Telegram channel. It is suspected that the balloons may have transported spies. According to their government, they cannot seize Russian assets in the country for the reconstruction of Ukraine. A working group commissioned by the government had concluded that the non-repayable expropriation of private assets of legitimate origin under Swiss law was not permissible, as stated in a statement published on Wednesday. Switzerland had opposed EU sanctions almost a year after Russia's attack on Ukraine. Since then, the country has frozen Russian assets in billions. In view of international proposals to seize them and to use the money for the reconstruction of Ukraine, one working group of the Ministry of Justice had asked to clarify the legality of such a procedure. This has now explained that the inclusion of frozen private assets is incompatible with the Federal Constitution and the applicable legal order and violates the international obligations of Switzerland.An analysis by the British research institute IISS on global army stocks shows the drastic losses of the Russian armed forces – and which Asian country supplies particularly many weapons. Read about the war in which hundreds of children have died after a year. Between February 2022 and January 2023, at least 438 children were killed by warfare and 842 were injured, the organization informed. Distrusted pedestrians and landmines also threatened children. Many families were also in need due to destroyed infrastructure. Within the country, around 3.3 million children and young people are dependent on acute humanitarian support. According to Unicef estimates, 16 million people have no safe access to water and sanitation facilities. "We must not allow children and young people in Ukraine to become a lost generation," said Georg Graf Waldersee, Chairman of Unicef, according to the communication. The organization has expanded its aid in Ukraine since the beginning of the war. In addition to access to clean water and health services for several million people, education and psychosocial assistance were provided to children and young people. A total of EUR 103 million has been donated from Germany to date.The Ukrainian Defence Minister Olexij Resnikow is expected to remain in office surprisingly. He had been asked by President to stay in office, said Resnikow in an interview with the news agency on the question whether he will continue to be in office in the coming months as Minister of Defence. "Yes, that was my president's decision," says Resnikow. Recently there were speculations about the future of Resnikow. A member of Selenskyi's party had declared, following cooruptions, that Resnikov would be replaced and other European states will provide military equipment through an international fund, including spare parts for tanks and artillery ammunition. That's what the British Ministry of Defense tells us. The country has therefore agreed with the Netherlands, , , , and to allow Ukraine to receive a support package worth around EUR 225 million. In concrete terms, the Commission proposes restrictions on dozens of electronic components used in Russian weapons systems such as drones, missiles and helicopters, she said in Strasbourg. Machine parts and spare parts for trucks and jet engines are also included.Russia also includes hundreds of drones of Iranian design in Ukraine, explained by the Leyen. It is therefore suggested that Iranian companies should also be targeted, including those close to the Revolutionary Guard. It is specifically about dual-use goods. So-called dual-use guards can be used both civilian and military. Seven bodies from this should be affected by restrictions. For them there was a complete ban on selling certain goods to Russia, said of the Leyen.In view of the Russian war against the neighboring country, invests in its arms production. The State will provide the Huta Stalowa Wola Group with around EUR 125 million of recapitalisation funds, the Prime Minister announced according to the PAP agency. It's just the first tranche. Further three-digit millions are to follow. "We are creating a new, enormous production potential in our country", said the national conservative politician.Huta Stalowa Wola, based in Southeast Poland, is largely owned by the state. The industrial complex was founded with a steelworks before World War II. Among the products are today the self-propelled AHS Kryl with a 155-millimeter artillery gun, the AHS crab armor and the multiple rocket thrower WR-40 Langusta. The discussions on a tenth European Union sanction package are a volume of eleven billion euros, according to the EU Commission's President. "We need to maintain pressure," says Leyen in the European Parliament, shortly before the start of the consultations of representatives of the EU states. This is about export restrictions for dual-use electronic components used in Russian weapons systems such as drones, missiles and helicopters. For the first time, Russia sanctions could also be directed against them because the Islamic Republic supported the war against Ukraine. For Russia uses hundreds of drones produced in Iran in Ukraine. According to EU wars, it is also planned to exclude four other Russian banks from the international payment system Swift. In addition, rubber and asphalt should be placed on the list of blocked imports from Russia.The British Minister of Defence Ben Wallace has significantly dampened the hopes of rapid fighter jet deliveries. "I don't think we're going to deliver fighter jets in the coming months or even years, because these are quite different weapon systems than tank defense missiles," said Wallace in an interview. It takes a very long time to learn how to fly with such jets, and a whole crew will be needed, like a Formula 1 team. "And, we will not send 200 members of the Royal Air Force to Ukraine in wartimes," said Wallace.The Ukrainian President had called for urgent combat jets to defend the Russian aggression during his recent visit to the Russian Federation. In principle, the British Prime Minister will examine whether combat aircraft are available to Ukraine. However, he also spoke of a “long-term” solution. Even in spring, the first Ukrainian pilots could be trained in -Jets in the UK. In an interview with the BBC, Wallace also stressed how strong the Russian army was demanded by the war in Ukraine. "We currently estimate that 97 percent of the Russian army, the whole Russian army, is in Ukraine."They want to go more strongly against banks with connections. "We are looking at other banks and financial institutions to see how Russia is financially linked to the outside world," said James O'Brien, in the U.S. Department of State for the Coordination of Sanctions, the news agency. "It is possible that there will be more measures."The West had blocked access to Russian monetary houses to the international SWIFT payment system after Russia's invasion of Ukraine. The largest Russian institutes such as Sberbank and VTB had to stop their operation to a large extent. Western governments also freed Russian central bank reserves of $300 billion. However, not all connections have been separated: institutions such as Unicredit and Raiffeisen Bank International continue their business on the Russian market and have to adhere to regulations. title: Schwangerschaftsabbrüche: Virginia will Auswertung von Menstruationsdaten verbieten
subtitle: Der Senat in Virginia wollte mit einem Gesetz die Auswertung von Menstruationsdaten verbieten. Der Schritt war als Schutzmaßnahme für Frauen gedacht. Doch der republikanische Gouverneur Glenn Youngkin stellt sich quer.
content: In Virginia soll die Auswertung von Menstruationsdaten durch Strafbehörden, etwa im Fall eines Schwangerschaftsabbruches, auch in Zukunft weiter möglich bleiben. Wie mehrere Medien, darunter die »«, berichten, blockierte der republikanische Gouverneur des Bundesstaates, Glenn Youngkin, ein Gesetz, das vorsah, die Evaluation dieser Gesundheitsdaten in dem Staat zu verbieten.Eigentlich war das Vorhaben der Demokraten sogar von der Hälfte der Republikaner im Senat des Staates unterstützt worden. Es hätte den Behörden künftig verboten, die Daten der Frauen etwa über Tracking-Apps auf Telefonen auszuwerten, selbst bei einem Durchsuchungsbeschluss.Die Befürworter des Gesetzes argumentieren, dass die Informationen genutzt würden, um Verstöße gegen das Abtreibungsgesetz zu ahnden.Doch der Republikaner Youngkin, der bereits ein Abtreibungsverbot ab der fünfzehnten Woche vorangetrieben hatte, verhinderte das Gesetz auf den letzten Metern mithilfe des von den Republikanern kontrollierten Repräsentantenhauses.Maggie Cleary, in Youngkins Administration für öffentliche Sicherheit zuständig, verwies darauf, dass unklar sei, inwiefern die Daten möglicherweise für künftige Ermittlungen benötigt würden. Ein solches Gesetz würde die Fähigkeit der Strafverfolgung bedrohen. Es sei nicht die Aufgabe des Gesetzgebers, den Umfang von Durchsuchungsbeschlüssen einzuschränken.»Derzeit sind alle Gesundheitsinformationen oder App-Informationen per Durchsuchungsbeschluss verfügbar. Und wir glauben, dass dies auch weiterhin der Fall sein sollte«, so Cleary.US-Gouverneur Glenn Youngkin bei einem Wahlkampfauftritt
en_title: Pregnancy Abruptions: Virginia wants to ban evaluation of menstruation data
en_content: In Virginia, the evaluation of menstruation data by criminal authorities, for example in the event of an abortion, should continue to be possible in the future. As several media, including the »«, report, the Republican Governor of the State, Glenn Youngkin, blocked a law that seemed to prohibit the evaluation of these health data in the state. In the future, it would have forbidden the authorities to evaluate the data of women, for example via tracking apps on telephones, even in a search decision.The advocates of the law argue that the information would be used to alleviate violations of the abortion law.However, the Republican Youngkin, who had already advanced a ban on abortion from the fifteenth week, would possibly prevent the law from investigating in the last few meters. Such a law would threaten the ability of prosecution. It is not the task of the legislator to limit the scope of search decisions. »In the meantime, all health information or app information is available by search.
